title: philotheca nodiflora subsp. calycina
intro: Philotheca nodiflora subsp. calycina, commonly known as the lemon-scented bottlebrush, is a plant species within the Rutaceae family. It is valued for its essential oils, which possess antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential candidate for various medicinal applications. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Philotheca nodiflora subsp. calycina can be used to create teas, essential oils, and tinctures for topical or internal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
